danger_signals.visual_form: Genre-appropriate hostile creatures (octopus in pirate film, spider in adventure film, undead/ghosts in horror film)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring avoidance or defeat; escalates tension through genre context

danger_signals.visual_form: Isometric perspective creating visual ambiguity around ledge placement and fall distances
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; difficulty judging safe passage through platforming sections

danger_signals.visual_form: Graveyard scenery, large staircase, cannon fire in pirate sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Thematic danger tied to film genre; communicates tone and threat type

safety_signals.meaning: Progress markers and resource gathering; 100 collected grants extra life (safety net)

safety_signals.visual_form: Five stars per level hidden in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Optional completion objectives; finding all unlocks bonus stages and better ending (progression safety/reward)

safety_signals.visual_form: Movement between distinct cinematic settings (pirate ship → canyon/temple → graveyard)
safety_signals.meaning: Checkpoint progression; safe passage to next stage indicates forward momentum

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bubble motif (origin tied to 7 Up soda bubble)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present across all film genre worlds; represents continuity as protagonist
Imprisoned within film projector; appear at ending to celebrate Spot's escape

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Genre-specific monster designs (giant octopus, giant spider, robot mech)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss enemies scale to epic proportions; different visual archetypes per film genre
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Inhabit each film world; chase Spot during final escape sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spot falls into projector, enters cinematic film worlds via screen transitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced progression through sequential film genre levels; no choice of level order

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pirate ship settings, nautical environment, octopus boss arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Platforming through ship decks; cannon riding sequences; boss encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Canyon landscapes, minecart ride visuals, temple architecture, giant spider arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exploration-based platforming; vehicle traversal segments; mid-stage boss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Graveyard scenery, large staircase architecture, dark atmospheric tone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vertical navigation puzzle; tension-building level design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Horror film loops; Spot recognizes incompletion and re-enters projector voluntarily
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game restart forced; Spot demonstrates self-awareness of incomplete state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Space bonus stage unlocked; final 'It's Full of Stars' level with spaceship and robot mech
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to true final boss; successful escape from projector; celebratory ending with all friends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Theater exit; monsters pursuing Spot and duplicated Spots; visual chaos during credits
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chase sequence; multiple Spot clones flee together; monsters seek revenge

environmental_storytelling.example: Each world is a distinct movie genre (pirate, adventure, horror, sci-fi, western, dinosaur) communicated entirely through environmental visuals and enemy design; plot progresses through genre transitions
Giant octopus = pirate threat; giant spider = adventure peril; robot mech = sci-fi climax; visual archetype immediately communicates level identity
Isometric graphics create movie-like viewing angle; reinforces 'trapped in film' narrative through visual language reminiscent of old cinema
Hidden stars unlock progression path; finding all five per level gates access to bonus stages and true ending; mechanical narrative gating
Final chase depicts environmental chaos (monsters pursuing, multiple Spots visible); visual intensity increases to communicate climactic state
Spot's companions appear trapped at story start; only visible again at ending after successful escape; spatial separation communicates stakes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses levels, collects dots and stars, progresses through film worlds sequentially
Voluntarily re-enters projector after noticing stars were missed; demonstrates narrative agency
Escapes theater; duplicates of Spot visible; flees from pursuing monsters
Attack Spot; occupy level-specific environments; represent genre-appropriate threats
Scale to epic proportions; serve as level finales; require defeat to progress
Remain trapped until game completion; appear in celebration at true ending; pursue escape alongside Spot
Chase Spot during theater escape; pursue multiple Spot clones; create final cinematic threat

notes: Spot Goes to Hollywood uses film genre as primary world-state communication mechanism. The game's narrative is entirely visual/environmental: Spot is trapped in a movie projector and must escape through sequential film worlds. Danger is signaled through genre-appropriate enemy design (pirates → octopus, adventure → spider, horror → undead/mech). Safety and progression are communicated via collectible red dots (extra lives) and hidden stars (unlock completion path). The isometric perspective intentionally blurs platform ledges, creating visual ambiguity as both narrative device (cinematic framing) and mechanical challenge. Incompletion is acknowledged by Spot's self-aware re-entry into projector, creating a meta-narrative loop. Victory unlocks space/sci-fi final stage and true ending with escape sequence. The game lacks explicit faction markers or political messaging; all world-state communication flows through cinematic/genre theming and boss design.
